summary: Ourchival is a private reference archive with browser capture, Convex metadata, Drive originals, and generated previews.

reviews.summary: Reviewed the origin-backed expansion from a basic capture vault into an authenticated, session-aware archive with reusable organization, explainable enrichment, and asynchronous media derivatives.

throughCommit.subject: Add first-class capture session bundles and import review (#41)

reviews.summary: Initial map established the browser-to-vault capture path, Convex catalog, portable Drive storage, Reliquary experience, shared contracts, and unresolved privacy boundary.

questions.question: What authenticates the private vault and capture endpoints?
questions.whyItMatters: Resolved at this scale: owner routes require a deployment key, capture accepts that key or a revocable paired-device token, and CORS no longer grants every origin.

questions.question: Which canonical identity prevents the same source asset from producing duplicate references?
questions.whyItMatters: Resolved in the committed capture path with an explicit precedence: exact original asset URL, normalized canonical URL, then source URL, while an existing record can receive missing context.

questions.question: Can the owner distinguish Drive originals, Convex fallbacks, and linked assets?
questions.whyItMatters: Resolved in Reliquary's inspector: the asset label names the provider while cards and details prefer private derivatives before falling back to the stored or linked original.

questions.question: When should capture sessions be written directly instead of reconstructed from recent successful references?
questions.whyItMatters: The current reconstruction creates a useful review group but cannot recover expected, duplicate, skipped, or failed counts from the extension checkpoint and is bounded to recent references.

questions.question: Which indexed representation should replace bounded relationship hydration when rare searches must cover the full archive?
questions.whyItMatters: Field-level explanations are useful, but the current newest-first application scan intentionally stops after a bounded number of candidates per response.

questions.question: How should heuristic version and threshold provenance remain visible after suggestions or relatedness rules change?
questions.whyItMatters: Current jobs preserve outcomes and reasons, but not the rule-set version that produced them, which limits reproducibility across future tuning.

questions.question: What should retry failed derivative jobs and reclaim blobs written before an interrupted completion mutation?
questions.whyItMatters: Failures are observable and force-retryable, but automatic retry policy and orphaned storage cleanup are not part of the committed worker boundary.

areas.name: Browser-to-vault capture pipeline
areas.purpose: Turns single items, tabs, pasted URLs, bookmarks, and rich multi-image posts into duplicate-safe, source-aware references with recoverable batch and session identity.
areas.verdict: Batch capture resumes after interruption. Session totals are still reconstructed from saved references.

walkthrough.title: Follow one interrupted multi-image capture into review
walkthrough.outcome: See how rich browser context becomes checkpointed requests, duplicate-safe records, and a reviewable capture group.

steps.label: Parse the source context
steps.explanation: The content script snapshots page metadata and delegates X article normalization to the shared parser.
steps.invariant: Every parsed media item points back to one canonical source post and retains creator or alt context when available.

steps.label: Checkpoint each attempted item
steps.explanation: The batch runner stamps one session ID, records successes and failures, advances the index, and persists state after each attempt.
steps.invariant: An interruption resumes after the last persisted item instead of replaying the whole batch.

steps.label: Authenticate and deduplicate capture
steps.explanation: The capture route authorizes the paired device, normalizes identity, enriches an existing match or inserts one new reference, and retains session identity.
steps.invariant: The same active asset or canonical source is not represented by a second reference.

steps.label: Reconstruct the review group
steps.explanation: Recent references are grouped by captureSessionId and upserted into an owner-only review ledger.
steps.invariant: A session's owner review state survives later synchronization.

areas.name: Convex catalog and relationships
areas.purpose: Stores references, assets, snapshots, capture sessions, boards, tags, projects, saved searches, enrichment work, suggestions, and aggregate archive counts.
areas.verdict: The data model covers the main archive objects. Some relationship queries still scan at personal-library scale.

areas.name: Portable original-file storage
areas.purpose: Keeps originals in private Google Drive when configured, records provider provenance, supports Convex or linked fallbacks, and supplies private originals to the derivative worker.
areas.changed: Secured and connected to derived media through origin/main
areas.verdict: Originals remain portable, while previews add a second storage path to operate.

areas.name: Reliquary vault experience
areas.purpose: Provides owner-gated inbox review, archive navigation, paging, filters, tags, boards, projects, saved searches, batch organization, enrichment review, related browsing, and capture-session review.
areas.changed: Recomposed into focused panels and hooks through origin/main
areas.verdict: The vault supports the full archive workflow, but its adjacent panels are becoming crowded.

areas.name: Shared capture and parsing contracts
areas.purpose: Keeps the capture payload, page snapshot, platform detection, and site-specific parsing reusable between extension, backend, and tests.
areas.changed: Rich metadata and the X parser landed through origin/main
areas.verdict: X has a dedicated parser. Other sites still use the generic page snapshot.

areas.name: Private owner and Clipper boundary
areas.purpose: Gates owner reads and mutations behind a deployment secret while granting capture-only browser access through expiring one-use pairing codes and revocable device tokens.
areas.changed: Implemented and reviewed through origin/main at 93229ca
areas.verdict: Owner and capture credentials are separated. Rotation and narrower device scopes are not implemented.

walkthrough.title: Pair and revoke one Clipper
walkthrough.outcome: Understand how the owner key delegates capture without being copied into the extension.

steps.label: Unlock as owner
steps.explanation: The web gate validates the owner key and attaches it to owner HTTP and Convex calls for the session.
steps.invariant: Owner-only routes never accept an unauthenticated request.

steps.label: Issue a short-lived grant
steps.explanation: An authenticated owner creates a hashed pairing grant with a ten-minute expiry.
steps.invariant: The displayed pairing secret is short-lived and usable at most once.

steps.label: Exchange for device authority
steps.explanation: The Clipper receives a random device token while Convex stores only its hash and device metadata.
steps.invariant: The extension never receives or persists the owner master key.

steps.label: Reject revoked capture
steps.explanation: Each capture supplies the device bearer token and surfaces a failed authorization in the persisted batch result.
steps.invariant: Revocation takes effect at the next server authorization check.

areas.name: Reusable organization and explainable retrieval
areas.purpose: Organizes one reference across tags, boards, and projects, preserves project-specific reuse context, supports bounded batch operations, and returns labeled reasons for search matches.
areas.changed: New durable area reviewed through origin/main at 93229ca
areas.verdict: References can be reused across projects and searches show match reasons. Full-archive search still uses bounded scans.

areas.name: Reviewable enrichment and similarity
areas.purpose: Tracks enrichment as observable jobs, proposes rather than silently applies taxonomy, and ranks related references with explicit structural, source, textual, and visual reasons.
areas.changed: New durable area reviewed through origin/main at 93229ca
areas.verdict: Suggested tags require approval. Heuristic versions and scan limits still need to be exposed.

areas.name: Asynchronous media derivative pipeline
areas.purpose: Claims stored originals for bounded Node processing into private WebP previews, thumbnails, dimensions, hashes, and dominant colors without replacing original-provider provenance.
areas.changed: New durable area reviewed through origin/main at 93229ca
areas.verdict: The worker creates browsing assets without moving originals. Retry and orphan cleanup are still missing.

walkthrough.title: Turn one private original into browsing assets
walkthrough.outcome: Trace queue idempotency, worker ownership, bounded image processing, and the final asset transition.

steps.label: Queue only eligible work
steps.explanation: The mutation requires a Drive or Convex original, reuses active work, and schedules a new internal job only when needed.
steps.invariant: One asset has at most one queued or running media-derivative job.

steps.label: Claim before decoding
steps.explanation: The Node action loads job context and atomically claims queued work before fetching the original.
steps.invariant: A worker that cannot claim the job performs no transformation.

steps.label: Bound and derive
steps.explanation: Sharp enforces input limits, orients the image, emits two WebPs, and derives hashes, dimensions, and colors.
steps.invariant: The archival original is read but never overwritten.

steps.label: Commit asset and job state
steps.explanation: The completion mutation validates the job-asset relationship, stores derivative metadata, and marks both asset and job ready.
steps.invariant: A ready asset points to both stored derivatives and a succeeded job record.
